question: Do large language models actually understand and reason like humans do?
topic: Addresses fundamental misconceptions about AI that affect public policy and individual decision-maki

discovery_sources.text: Current large language models can achieve gold medals in mathematics competitions
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: AI systems spontaneously develop survival as a sub-goal when trained on other objectives without explicit programming
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: AI systems can be trained to deliberately give wrong answers and will generalize this behavior beyond the training context
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: Large language models confabulate (create false memories) in ways similar to human memory recall
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: Current language models can perform reasoning tasks and detect logical inconsistencies in their own beliefs
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: Large language models and their reasoning capabilities
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: Confabulation versus hallucination in language models
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton
